intro: Koanophyllon peninsulare, also known as "Venezuelan ironwort" or "iron tree," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Venezuela and Colombia, classified as a member of the family Asteraceae. The plant is rich in iron and has been traditionally used to treat anemia, iron deficiency, and other health issues associated with iron deficiency, such as fatigue, weakness, and shortness of breath. Bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon peninsulare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koanophyllon peninsulare is commonly prepared as a dietary supplement, tea, or capsule, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its potential health benefits.
